seoTitle: Porongurup vs Port Denison — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Porongurup and Port Denison. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Porongurup vs Port Denison: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Porongurup and Port Denison.
heading: Porongurup vs Port Denison — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Porongurup, Western Australia and Port Denison, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Denison has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Porongurup (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Porongurup or Port Denison safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Porongurup has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Port Denison scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Porongurup scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Port Denison scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Porongurup or Port Denison?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Denison has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
